# launchdarkly_access_token (Resource)

Provides a LaunchDarkly access token resource.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "launchdarkly_access_token" "reader_token" {
  name = "Reader token managed by Terraform"
  role = "reader"
}

# With a custom role
resource "launchdarkly_access_token" "custom_role_token" {
  name         = "DevOps"
  custom_roles = ["ops"]
}

# With an inline custom role (policy statements)
resource "launchdarkly_access_token" "token_with_policy_statements" {
  name = "Integration service token"
  inline_roles {
    actions   = ["*"]
    effect    = "deny"
    resources = ["proj/*:env/production"]
  }
  service_token = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `custom_roles` (Set of String) Custom role IDs used as access limits.
- `default_api_version` (Number) Default API version for this token.
- `expire` (Number, Deprecated) Deprecated. Expiry epoch — setting it resets the token.
- `inline_roles` (Block List) Inline custom-role policy statements. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--inline_roles))
- `name` (String) Human-friendly name for the access token.
- `policy_statements` (Block List, Deprecated) Deprecated inline-role definition; use inline_roles. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--policy_statements))
- `role` (String) Built-in role: reader, writer, or admin.
- `service_token` (Boolean) Whether the token is a service token.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `token` (String, Sensitive) The plaintext token. Only exposed on create / reset.

<a id="nestedblock--inline_roles"></a>
### Nested Schema for `inline_roles`

Required:

- `effect` (String) Either `allow` or `deny`.

Optional:

- `actions` (List of String) The list of actions the statement applies to.
- `not_actions` (List of String) The list of actions the statement does not apply to.
- `not_resources` (List of String) The list of resource specifiers the statement does not apply to.
- `resources` (List of String) The list of resource specifiers the statement applies to.


<a id="nestedblock--policy_statements"></a>
### Nested Schema for `policy_statements`

Required:

- `effect` (String) Either `allow` or `deny`.

Optional:

- `actions` (List of String) The list of actions the statement applies to.
- `not_actions` (List of String) The list of actions the statement does not apply to.
- `not_resources` (List of String) The list of resource specifiers the statement does not apply to.
- `resources` (List of String) The list of resource specifiers the statement applies to.
